title: Accounts Payable Clerk
reason: AP automation via RPA and AI invoice matching (Tipalti, SAP) already handles 70%+ of tasks at large firms. 2 years is realistic for widespread 50% displacement.

title: Accounts Receivable Clerk
reason: AR automation tools (HighRadius, Billtrust) already deployed widely. Payment matching and dunning largely automated. 2 years is accurate.

title: Administrative Assistant
reason: LLM-powered email drafting, calendar AI, and workflow tools are eroding core tasks rapidly. 3 years accounts for SMB adoption lag.

title: Billing Specialist
reason: Automated billing platforms handle invoicing, payment tracking, and dispute flagging. Could be slightly faster than 3y but within range.

title: Bookkeeper
reason: AI-powered accounting software (Xero, QuickBooks AI features) automates categorization, reconciliation. Full bookkeeper role has compliance nuance slowing displacement to ~3y.

title: Junior Bookkeeper
reason: Entry-level bookkeeping (data entry, basic reconciliation) is almost entirely automatable with current tools. 2 years is on target.

title: Call Centre Agent
reason: Voice AI (Google CCAI, Amazon Connect) already handling 40-60% of inbound calls at major companies. Goldman Sachs and McKinsey flag this as near-term disruption. 2y is accurate.

title: Cashier
reason: Self-checkout and automated stores (Amazon Go model) expanding but physical retail adoption is slower than pure digital roles. Some retailers pulling back on self-checkout. 2-3y is the range.

title: Check Processing Clerk
reason: Check volumes declining 10%+ annually. Mobile deposit, OCR, and automated clearing already handle the majority. Effectively obsolete at many institutions.

title: Classified Ad Taker
reason: Role is functionally extinct. Online self-service platforms (Craigslist, Facebook Marketplace, Gumtree) eliminated this years ago. 1y or less is generous.

title: Correspondence Clerk
reason: LLMs generate, sort, and route correspondence efficiently. Template-based responses already automated. Could happen faster than 2y but within 3y window.

title: Data Entry Clerk
reason: OCR + LLM extraction + RPA makes this one of the most automatable roles per Oxford/Frey-Osborne (99% probability). Already largely displaced; 1-2y for remaining holdouts.

title: Desktop Publisher
reason: AI design tools (Canva AI, Adobe Firefly) automate layout and template work. Complex editorial design takes longer but basic DTP is already automated.

title: Document Scanner Operator
reason: Automated document feeding scanners with AI classification make this role redundant. Batch scanning with auto-indexing is standard. 1y is correct.

title: Executive Assistant
reason: High-touch relationship management, judgment calls, and complex coordination provide a buffer. AI handles scheduling and email triage but strategic gatekeeping is harder to automate. 4-5y range.

title: File Clerk
reason: Digital document management systems with AI tagging have made physical filing nearly obsolete. Most organizations already digitized. 1y is accurate.

title: Front Desk Receptionist
reason: AI kiosks and virtual receptionists handle check-in and routing, but physical presence still valued in many settings (medical, legal offices). 3y balances tech capability vs adoption.

title: Invoice Processor
reason: AI invoice processing (Kofax, ABBYY, Rossum) extracts, validates, and routes invoices with 95%+ accuracy. Already deployed at scale. 1y is correct.

title: Library Catalog Clerk
reason: Automated cataloging via OCLC, AI metadata extraction, and RFID systems have largely replaced manual cataloging. Role already shrinking significantly.

title: Mail Room Clerk
reason: Physical mail handling has a robotics barrier. While digital mail (scanning services like Earth Class Mail) reduces volume, physical sorting still needs humans. 1-2y depending on mail volume trends.

title: Medical Transcriptionist
reason: AI speech-to-text (Nuance DAX, Amazon Transcribe Medical) already handles medical dictation with high accuracy. AHDI reported 50%+ job losses since 2020. Effectively automated now.

title: Meter Reader
reason: Smart meters (AMI) transmit readings automatically. US smart meter penetration exceeded 75% by 2025. Role is disappearing rapidly. 1y is correct.

title: Office Administrator
reason: Broader role with vendor management, compliance, and facilities oversight provides buffer beyond pure admin tasks. AI handles documentation and scheduling but coordination takes longer. 4y is reasonable.

title: Office Manager
reason: People management, conflict resolution, and culture-building are hard to automate. AI handles operational tasks but leadership component extends timeline. 4-5y range.

title: Order Clerk
reason: E-commerce and EDI systems already automate order processing. Remaining manual order entry for phone/fax orders is shrinking fast. 2y is accurate.

title: Payroll Specialist
reason: Payroll software (ADP, Gusto) automates calculations but compliance, tax variations, and exception handling require human oversight. Regulatory complexity buffers the role. 3y is accurate.

title: Postal Sorter
reason: Automated sorting machines with OCR and AI handle 95%+ of mail sorting at major postal services. Human sorters handle exceptions only. Already mostly automated.

title: Print Shop Operator
reason: Digital printing with automated prepress, color management, and job queuing reduces operator role. Physical machine tending provides some buffer. 2y is reasonable.

title: Proofreader
reason: Grammarly, GPT-based proofreading, and AI editing tools handle grammar, style, and consistency. Nuanced editorial judgment for high-stakes content takes longer. 2y is accurate for 50% threshold.

title: Receptionist
reason: Virtual receptionist AI and automated check-in kiosks are mainstream, but physical presence for visitor management in offices slows full displacement. 4-5y is the right range.

title: Scheduling Coordinator
reason: AI scheduling tools (Calendly AI, x.ai, healthcare scheduling AI) already handle complex multi-party scheduling. 2-3y depending on industry. Within range.

title: Switchboard Operator
reason: Role is functionally extinct. VoIP, auto-attendants, and AI call routing replaced switchboards decades ago. 1y is generous but fine for the site's purposes.

title: Telephone Operator
reason: Similar to switchboard operator — automated systems handle call routing, directory assistance, and operator services. Mostly gone already. Within 3y window.

title: Title Examiner
reason: AI can search title records and flag issues, but legal liability and state-specific regulations require human sign-off. Doma and similar PropTech companies automating but adoption is gradual. 2-3y range.

title: Toll Booth Operator
reason: Electronic tolling (E-ZPass, Linkt in Australia) has eliminated most toll booth operators already. Role is functionally obsolete in most developed countries.

title: Transcriptionist
reason: AI transcription (Whisper, Otter.ai, Rev AI) achieves 95%+ accuracy. Human transcriptionists now mainly handle edge cases. 1y is accurate.

title: Typist
reason: Dedicated typist role is essentially extinct. Voice-to-text, LLM drafting, and everyone typing their own work eliminated this role. Already past 50% automation.

title: Word Processor Operator
reason: LLMs draft, format, and edit documents. Dedicated word processing pools disappeared at most organizations. 1-2y for remaining niche uses. Within range.

title: Accountant
reason: AI handles bookkeeping, categorization, and basic analysis, but professional judgment, audit defense, and advisory work extend the timeline. CPA regulatory requirements add friction. 4-5y range.

title: Actuary
reason: AI excels at pattern recognition in mortality/risk data, but regulatory model validation, novel risk assessment, and professional certification requirements slow displacement. 5-6y range.

title: Auditor
reason: AI handles transaction sampling, anomaly detection, and compliance checking. But professional skepticism, client interviews, and regulatory sign-off requirements extend the timeline. 4-5y.

title: Claims Adjuster
reason: AI image analysis and NLP assess claims data, but physical inspections, fraud investigation, and negotiation require human judgment. McKinsey estimates 40-50% of claims tasks automatable by 2027-2028.

title: Claims Processor
reason: Straight-through processing via AI already handles simple claims at major insurers. Data entry and validation are largely automated. 2y is accurate for 50% of core tasks.

title: Compliance Officer
reason: RegTech AI monitors transactions and flags issues, but interpretation of regulations, judgment calls, and regulatory relationships require humans. Heavy regulatory oversight of the role itself slows automation. 6-7y.

title: Conveyancer
reason: AI handles title searches and document preparation, but property law varies by jurisdiction and transactions require human sign-off. PropTech automating parts but legal liability slows adoption. 5y is accurate.

title: Credit Analyst
reason: AI credit scoring and financial statement analysis already widespread. Complex commercial credit analysis and relationship lending provide buffer. 3y is reasonable for 50% threshold.

title: Financial Analyst
reason: AI generates reports, runs models, and synthesizes data. But strategic interpretation, stakeholder communication, and novel situation analysis extend timeline. Goldman Sachs estimates 30% of finance tasks automatable near-term. 5y is reasonable.

title: Financial Planner
reason: Robo-advisors handle portfolio allocation, but holistic financial planning involves life goals, emotional coaching, and complex tax situations. Trust barrier is high. Our 4y may be slightly aggressive but within 3y window.

title: Freight Broker
reason: Digital freight platforms (Uber Freight, Convoy) automate matching but relationship management and exception handling remain human. Market fragmentation slows adoption. 3-4y range.

title: Insurance Agent
reason: AI chatbots and comparison sites erode simple policy sales, but complex commercial insurance, relationship selling, and regulatory requirements protect the role. 6y is well-calibrated.

title: Insurance Underwriter
reason: AI underwriting models (Lemonade, Root) already handle personal lines. Complex commercial and specialty underwriting takes longer. McKinsey flags underwriting as high automation potential. 4y is accurate.

title: Lawyer
reason: AI handles document review, legal research, and contract drafting, but courtroom advocacy, client counseling, regulatory barriers, and professional liability slow displacement significantly. WEF and Oxford both flag long timeline. 8y is well-calibrated.

title: Legal Secretary
reason: AI handles document formatting, calendar management, and correspondence. Court filing systems becoming more automated. But legal-specific knowledge and attorney preferences provide some buffer. 3y is accurate.

title: Loan Officer
reason: AI handles pre-qualification and documentation, but relationship lending, complex situations, and regulatory requirements (ECOA, fair lending) maintain human role. 4y is reasonable.

title: Loan Processor
reason: Document collection, verification, and compliance checking heavily automated by mortgage tech (Blend, ICE Mortgage). 2y is accurate for 50% displacement.

title: Mortgage Broker
reason: Online comparison tools and AI pre-approval reduce the broker's role, but complex situations and relationship trust provide buffer. 3-4y range, within window.

title: Mortgage Processor
reason: Very similar to loan processor — document assembly and verification highly automatable. Could be faster than 3y but within window.

title: Paralegal
reason: AI legal research (CoCounsel, Harvey) and document review tools automate core paralegal tasks. But complex case management and attorney support extend timeline slightly. 3-4y range.

title: Patent Examiner
reason: Prior art search is AI-accelerated, but patentability analysis requires deep technical judgment across diverse fields. Government employment and regulatory framework slow adoption. USPTO is cautious with AI. 5y is more realistic than 3y.

title: Risk Analyst
reason: AI risk models are powerful for quantitative risk, but qualitative risk assessment, scenario planning, and regulatory interpretation require human judgment. 5y is well-calibrated.

title: Stock Analyst
reason: AI generates financial models and screens stocks, but client relationships, novel thesis generation, and market narrative require humans. Quant funds demonstrate AI capability but sell-side analysis has trust barriers. 3-4y, within range.

title: Tax Preparer
reason: AI tax software (TurboTax AI, H&R Block) handles standard returns. Complex tax situations and audit representation extend the timeline. 3y is accurate for 50% of the role.

title: Assembly Line Worker
reason: Industrial robotics advanced but physical dexterity for varied tasks remains challenging. Automotive assembly highly automated but other manufacturing lags. Capital costs slow adoption at SMBs. 2-3y range.

title: Directory Assistance Operator
reason: Functionally extinct. Automated directory services and internet search replaced this role. Google and Siri handle inquiries. Already past 50% automation.

title: Factory Quality Inspector
reason: Computer vision QC systems deployed widely in electronics and automotive. But varied product lines and regulatory inspections (food, pharma) need human oversight. 2-3y range.

title: Map Drafter
reason: GIS software, satellite imagery, and AI mapping tools (Google Maps, ESRI AI) have automated cartographic production. Manual map drafting is essentially gone. 1y is correct.

title: Drafter
reason: AI-assisted CAD (Autodesk AI, generative design) automates routine drafting but engineering judgment, client specifications, and regulatory compliance require human oversight. Complex drafting has longer runway than simple map drafting. 4y is accurate.

title: Parking Lot Attendant
reason: Automated parking systems (ANPR, ticketless parking) widespread. Some physical security/assistance role remains. 2y is reasonable for 50% displacement.

title: Photo Lab Technician
reason: Digital photography eliminated most photo lab work. Remaining specialty printing is highly automated. Role has been declining for 15+ years. Essentially obsolete.

title: Production Planner
reason: AI-powered ERP and MRP systems optimize scheduling and inventory, but supply chain disruptions and cross-functional coordination require human judgment. 3y is accurate.

title: Telemarketer
reason: AI voice agents and robocalling already handle high-volume outbound sales. Oxford/Frey-Osborne rated this 99% automatable. Regulatory crackdowns on robocalls are the main constraint. 1y is accurate.

title: Telephone Survey Interviewer
reason: AI voice survey tools and online survey platforms have largely replaced phone-based surveying. Response rates for phone surveys have plummeted. 1y is correct.

title: Textile Worker
reason: Textile automation advanced in spinning and weaving but sewing/garment assembly remains difficult for robots due to fabric handling. Sewbots making progress but deployment is gradual. 2-3y range.

title: Ticket Agent
reason: Online booking, self-service kiosks, and AI chatbots handle most ticketing. Airport and transit ticket agents declining rapidly. 1y is accurate.

title: Travel Agent
reason: OTAs and AI trip planners handle standard bookings. But complex itineraries, group travel, and luxury/niche markets sustain human agents. Travel agent numbers stabilized after initial disruption. 2-3y for 50%.

title: Travel Booking Agent
reason: Simple booking is already automated by OTAs and corporate travel platforms. Less complex than full travel agent role. 1y is accurate.

title: Quantity Surveyor
reason: AI-powered BIM and cost estimation tools (CostX, Buildxact) automate takeoffs and pricing, but site visits, contract negotiation, and construction variability require human expertise. Construction industry is slow to adopt tech. 5y is well-calibrated.
